questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Which interval notation represents the set of all real numbers greater than 5?
questions.explanation: Greater than 5 means 5 is not included, so use parenthesis: (5, ∞). Option A includes 5, C is numbers ≤5, D is numbers <5.

explanations_tiered.definition: Interval notation (a, ∞) represents all real numbers greater than a, excluding a.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Parenthesis indicates endpoint not included; bracket indicates included.
explanations_tiered.beginner: Greater than 5 means numbers like 5.1, 6, 10... but not 5 itself. So use parenthesis: (5, ∞).
explanations_tiered.intermediate: x > 5 is written as (5, ∞) because 5 is excluded (parenthesis) and infinity always uses parenthesis.
explanations_tiered.advanced: ∞ is not a real number, so it always gets a parenthesis. Since 5 is not included, use parenthesis: (5, ∞).
misconceptions: Direction of inequality reversed: (-∞,5) is x<5.

visual_system.visual_description: Number line from 0 to 10 with open circle at 5 and arrow to the right.
visual_system.caption: Figure: All numbers greater than 5.
visual_system.accessibility_alt_text: Number line with open circle at 5, shading to the right.

publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. Option A includes 5, C is x≤5, D is x<5.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In the exponential expression \(2^5\), what is the exponent?
questions.hint: The exponent is the small number written above and to the right.
questions.explanation: In \(2^5\), 2 is the base and 5 is the exponent (power). Option A is the base; C is 2×5; D is the value of \(2^5 = 32\).

source_trace.content_anchor: α is the base and n is the exponent or power

explanations_tiered.definition: The exponent (or power) is the small superscript number indicating how many times the base is multiplied by itself.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: In aⁿ, a is the base, n is the exponent. \(2^5\) has exponent 5.
explanations_tiered.beginner: 2⁵ means 2×2×2×2×2. The small 5 on top is the exponent, telling how many 2's to multiply.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: In the expression \(2^5\), 5 is the exponent (power) and 2 is the base.
explanations_tiered.advanced: Exponent notation: \(2^5 = 2 × 2 × 2 × 2 × 2\). The exponent 5 is the number of factors.
reasoning_steps: Recall definition: aⁿ has base a, exponent n.

publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Distinguish between base, exponent, and value.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: Identify base and exponent in exponential expressions
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. Exponent is 5. Option A is base, C is product, D is value.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Round 10.673 to one decimal place.
questions.hint: Look at the second decimal digit to decide rounding.
questions.explanation: To round to 1 decimal place, look at the hundredths digit (7). Since 7 ≥ 5, round the tenths digit up: 10.6 becomes 10.7. Option A rounds down; C and D have two decimal places.

calculation_steps: Identify tenths digit (6) and hundredths digit (7).

explanations_tiered.definition: Rounding to one decimal place means keeping one digit after the decimal point.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Look at the second decimal digit; if 5 or more, increase the first decimal digit by 1.
explanations_tiered.beginner: 10.673 rounded to 1 decimal place: look at hundredths digit 7 (≥5), so round tenths 6 up to 7 → 10.7.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Tenths digit = 6, hundredths digit = 7. Since 7 ≥ 5, round up: 10.6 → 10.7.
reasoning_steps: Locate digit in first decimal place.
misconceptions: Rounding to 10.67 (two decimal places, not one).

glossary_entry.definition: Approximating to a specified number of digits after decimal.

publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Practice with different numbers, varying the rounding digit.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: Round numbers to given decimal places
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. Hundredths digit 7≥5 rounds tenths 6→7 → 10.7.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Express 1,856,700,000 in scientific notation.
questions.hint: Move decimal to get one digit before decimal, count places moved.
questions.explanation: 1,856,700,000 = 1.8567 × 10⁹ because decimal moves 9 places left. Option A moves 8 places, C moves 10, D coefficient not between 1 and 10.

explanations_tiered.definition: Scientific notation writes numbers as a × 10ⁿ with 1 ≤ a < 10 and integer n.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: For large numbers, count how many places the decimal moves left; that's the positive exponent.
explanations_tiered.beginner: 1,856,700,000 = 1.8567 × 10⁹. Move decimal 9 places left to get 1.8567.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Decimal moved 9 places left from 1,856,700,000 to 1.8567, so exponent = 9.
worked_solution: Step 1: Place decimal after first digit: 1.856700000
Step 2: Count digits after first digit: 9 digits
reasoning_steps: Move decimal to after first non-zero digit.
misconceptions: Counting wrong: 1,856,700,000 has 10 digits? Actually 1.8567×10⁹ means 10-digit number.
Forgetting to drop trailing zeros in coefficient.

publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Count zeros after first digit to get exponent.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: Express large numbers in scientific notation

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Rationalize the denominator of \(\frac{3}{\sqrt{5} - \sqrt{3}}\).
questions.hint: Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate \(\sqrt{5} + \sqrt{3}\).
questions.explanation: Multiply by conjugate: \(\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3}} = \frac{3(\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3})}{5 - 3} = \frac{3(\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{3})}{2}\). Option A uses wrong sign; C wrong denominator; D missing factor 3/2.

explanations_tiered.definition: Rationalizing a denominator with a binomial radical uses the conjugate to eliminate radicals.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate (same terms, opposite sign).
explanations_tiered.beginner: Multiply top and bottom by √5+√3. Denominator becomes (√5)²-(√3)²=5-3=2. Numerator = 3(√5+√3).
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Conjugate of (√5-√3) is (√5+√3). Multiply: denominator = 5-3=2, numerator = 3(√5+√3).
explanations_tiered.advanced: Using difference of squares: (√5-√3)(√5+√3)=2. So fraction = 3(√5+√3)/2.
worked_solution: Step 2: Multiply numerator and denominator by conjugate
reasoning_steps: Multiply numerator and denominator by conjugate.
misconceptions: Using conjugate with wrong sign (√5-√3 again).
Forgetting to multiply numerator by conjugate as well.

glossary_entry.definition: Using conjugate to eliminate radicals from denominator.

publishing_metadata.glossary_definition: Binomial with opposite sign between terms.
publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Always check denominator becomes integer after using conjugate.
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. Multiply by (√5+√3)/(√5+√3): denominator=2, numerator=3(√5+√3).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Three strings of lengths 78 cm, 117 cm, and 351 cm are cut into equal pieces. What is the greatest possible length of each piece?
questions.hint: The greatest length that divides all three numbers evenly is their GCF.
questions.explanation: The greatest possible length must divide 78, 117, and 351. GCF(78,117,351) = 39 because 78=2×3×13, 117=3²×13, 351=3³×13. Common factors: 3×13=39. Options B, C, D divide but are not greatest.

source_trace.content_anchor: strings 78,117,351 cm cut into equal lengths, find greatest possible length

explanations_tiered.definition: The greatest possible equal length that divides all measurements is the Greatest Common Factor (GCF).
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Find prime factors of each length; product of common primes to smallest powers gives GCF.
explanations_tiered.beginner: Find largest number that divides 78, 117, and 351. Try 39: 78÷39=2, 117÷39=3, 351÷39=9. Works perfectly.
explanations_tiered.advanced: GCF(78,117,351) = 39 because 39|78, 39|117, 39|351 and no larger integer divides all three (since 78/39=2, 117/39=3, 351/39=9 are coprime).
worked_solution: Step 4: Common primes: 3 (min exponent 1) and 13 (min exponent 1)
reasoning_steps: Find prime factors of each number.
For each common prime, take smallest exponent.

glossary_entry.definition: Using GCF to find largest equal divisor in measurement problems.

flashcard.front: Greatest equal piece length from 78,117,351 cm strings?

publishing_metadata.flashcard_front: Greatest piece length from 78,117,351 cm strings?
publishing_metadata.glossary_definition: Largest number dividing all given numbers.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: Apply GCF to real-world measurement problems
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer A. GCF=39. Options B=13, C=26, D=3 are common divisors but not greatest.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
questions.question: Which of the following numbers is prime?
questions.hint: A prime number has exactly two distinct factors: 1 and itself.
questions.explanation: 2 is prime because its only factors are 1 and 2. Option A: 1 has only one factor; C: 9=3×3 (three factors); D: 15=3×5 (four factors).

source_trace.section_anchor: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
source_trace.content_anchor: 2 is the only even prime number

explanations_tiered.definition: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 with exactly two distinct positive divisors: 1 and itself.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: 2 is prime because its only divisors are 1 and 2.
explanations_tiered.beginner: 2 can only be divided evenly by 1 and 2, so it's prime. 1 is not prime (only one factor). 9 and 15 have more factors.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Check each: 1 has one factor (not prime). 2 has factors {1,2} → prime. 9=3² has factors 1,3,9 → composite. 15=3×5 has factors 1,3,5,15 → composite.
explanations_tiered.advanced: By definition, 2 is prime. 1 is neither prime nor composite. 9 and 15 are composite as they have non-trivial divisors.
reasoning_steps: Recall prime definition: exactly two factors.
misconceptions: Thinking 1 is prime (has only one factor, not two).
Believing all odd numbers are prime (9,15 are counterexamples).
explanations_tiered.revision_note: 2 is the only even prime number.

glossary_entry.definition: Natural number >1 with exactly two factors.

publishing_metadata.revision_note: 2 is prime; 1 is not; 9,15 are composite.
publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Emphasize 1 is neither prime nor composite.
publishing_metadata.workbook_section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer B. 2 is prime. 1 has one factor; 9=3², 15=3×5 are composite.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
questions.question: Which of the following numbers is divisible by both 2 and 9?
questions.hint: Divisible by 2 means even. Divisible by 9 means sum of digits divisible by 9.
questions.explanation: 3,186 is even (ends with 6) and digit sum 3+1+8+6=18, divisible by 9. 2,334 sum=12 not multiple of 9; 42,435 not even; 5,712 sum=15 not multiple of 9.

source_trace.section_anchor: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers

explanations_tiered.definition: Divisible by 2: last digit even. Divisible by 9: sum of digits multiple of 9.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Check both conditions. Only numbers satisfying both are divisible by 18 (LCM of 2 and 9).
explanations_tiered.beginner: Even numbers: 3,186, 2,334, 5,712 (ends in even). Sum digits: 3,186 sum=18 (÷9), others not. So 3,186 works.
explanations_tiered.intermediate: Test each: 3,186 even, sum=18 (9×2). 2,334 even but sum=12 (not ÷9). 42,435 odd (fail 2). 5,712 even but sum=15 (not ÷9).
explanations_tiered.advanced: A number divisible by both 2 and 9 is divisible by LCM(2,9)=18. 3,186÷18=177 exactly.
worked_solution: Step 2: Compute digit sum for each even number
Step 3: Check if digit sum divisible by 9
reasoning_steps: Among evens, apply divisibility by 9 (sum digits ÷9).
explanations_tiered.revision_note: Divisible by 2 and 9 → divisible by 18. 3,186 works.

glossary_entry.definition: Number even and digit sum multiple of 9.

flashcard.front: Which number is divisible by both 2 and 9: 3,186; 2,334; 42,435; 5,712?

explanations_tiered.workbook_section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
explanations_tiered.solution_manual_note: Answer A. 3,186 even and digit sum 18. Option B sum=12, C odd, D sum=15.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
questions.question: What is the prime factorization of 252?

source_trace.section_anchor: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers

explanations_tiered.definition: Prime factorization is expressing a number as a product of prime numbers.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Divide by smallest primes repeatedly until 1 is reached.
reasoning_steps: Continue dividing until quotient is odd.
Collect all prime factors with exponents.

glossary_entry.definition: Product of prime numbers equal to the original number.

publishing_metadata.teacher_note: Use factor tree to visualize division steps.
publishing_metadata.learning_objective_tag: Express composite numbers in prime factorization form
publishing_metadata.workbook_section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer A. 252=2²×3²×7. Option B has 2³, C has 3³, D has 7².

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
questions.subsection: 1.1.5 Greatest common factor and least common multiple
questions.question: Find the LCM of 14 and 21.
questions.hint: List multiples or use prime factorization: 14=2×7, 21=3×7, LCM=2×3×7=42.
questions.explanation: Prime factors: 14=2×7, 21=3×7. LCM takes highest powers: 2¹×3¹×7¹=42. Multiples of 14:14,28,42...; of 21:21,42...; smallest common is 42. Option A is GCF, B is one number, D is a common multiple but not least.

calculation_steps: Take highest power of each prime: 2¹, 3¹, 7¹

source_trace.section_anchor: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
source_trace.subsection_anchor: 1.1.5 Greatest common factor and least common multiple

explanations_tiered.definition: Least Common Multiple (LCM) is the smallest positive integer that is a multiple of both numbers.
explanations_tiered.concept_summary: Find by prime factorization: product of highest powers of all primes appearing.
reasoning_steps: List prime factors of each number.
For each prime, take the largest exponent present.

glossary_entry.definition: Smallest number divisible by both given numbers.

publishing_metadata.workbook_section: 1.1 Revision on Natural Numbers and Integers
publishing_metadata.solution_manual_note: Answer C. LCM=42. Option A=GCF, B=21, D=84 (multiple but not least).
